plan_description: Scans Google Docs in the Drive folder 'Contracts', extracts contract end/expiration dates, and emails an HTML summary table of contracts ending within the next 30 days (including documents where an end date could not be found).

data: - Search Google Drive within the folder named “Contracts” for Google Docs.
- Restrict the candidate documents to those whose Google Doc title contains at least one of these keywords: “Contract”, “Agreement”, “MSA”, “SOW”, “Order Form”, “Statement of Work”.
- For each candidate Google Doc, retrieve the document text needed to locate end/expiration dates and counterparty names.
- Use today’s date as the reference date for all “next 30 days” calculations.
actions: - For each candidate contract document, extract the contract end/expiration date by checking multiple patterns, including:
- If the document contains an explicit label (for example: “End Date: …” or “Expiration Date: …”), extract the date value that follows the label.
- If the document contains a term sentence (for example: “This Agreement ends on …”), extract the date from that sentence.
- If the document contains a renewal/auto-renewal clause that includes an end date, extract the end date from that clause.
- If multiple plausible end/expiration dates are found in a single document, choose the most likely contract end date and record a note explaining that multiple dates were detected.

- Attempt to extract a counterparty name from the document (for example: the first party name block near the top, or a line containing “between … and …”); if not found, leave it blank and add a note if helpful.
- Compute “days remaining” as (end_date - today) in days when an end date is available.
- Filter results to include only contracts with an end date between today and today + 30 days (inclusive).
- If an end date cannot be found for a candidate contract document, include the document in the report with end date blank and a note indicating the end date was not found.
output: - Produce a single HTML table (embedded in the email body) containing one row per included document.
- Include these columns in the table: Document title, Document link, Counterparty, End date, Days remaining, Notes.
- Sort the table rows by end date ascending when an end date exists; place missing end-date rows at the bottom.
delivery: - Use a clear subject line indicating it is a contract end-date summary for the next 30 days.

- If no contracts are found ending within the next 30 days, send an email stating that no upcoming contract ends were detected, and (if any) include a short list of documents where an end date could not be found.
processing_steps: - Build the candidate document list from the Drive folder “Contracts” and the title keyword filter.
- Read each candidate Google Doc’s text.
- Extract end/expiration date and counterparty (when possible).
- Compute days remaining and apply the next-30-days filter.
- Generate the HTML table and email it to the user.

resolved_user_inputs.value: Contract, Agreement, MSA, SOW, Order Form, Statement of Work
